title: Engineering Sprint Planning - Sprint 24
transcript_text:
[Sarah Chen] (00:00:15): Good morning everyone. Let's review the Q3 roadmap and plan Sprint 24. The API migration is our top priority this sprint.
[John Park] (00:00:32): I can handle the API integration. I'll need access to the staging environment first though. Can DevOps provision that by tomorrow?
[Sarah Chen] (00:00:48): I'll ping Tom about staging access. Maria, can you coordinate the frontend changes with John's API work?
[Maria Lopez] (00:01:05): Sure, but I'm also working on the dashboard redesign. That's already behind schedule. Someone should look into the auth module too - there are three open security tickets.
[David Kim] (00:01:22): I'll take the auth module. But the deadline seems tight - can we push the dashboard redesign to next sprint?
[Sarah Chen] (00:01:40): Let's keep the dashboard in this sprint but reduce scope. Maria, focus on the critical user flows only. We can polish in Sprint 25. Alex, I need the load testing report by Wednesday.
[Alex Rivera] (00:02:00): Wednesday is tough with the production incident follow-up still pending. Can I get it to you by Thursday?
[Sarah Chen] (00:02:15): Thursday noon, final. No extensions. Let's also schedule a sync between John and Maria for Tuesday to align on the API contract before any frontend work begins.
[John Park] (00:02:35): Makes sense. I'll have the API spec draft ready by Monday EOD so Maria can review before the Tuesday sync.
[Maria Lopez] (00:02:50): That works. I'll block Tuesday 2-3 PM for the sync. One more thing - the design team hasn't finalized the new component library yet. Should I use the old components or wait?
[Sarah Chen] (00:03:10): Use the existing components for now. We can migrate to the new library in the next sprint. Let's not block on that. David, any dependencies for the auth work?
[David Kim] (00:03:28): I need the security audit report from last quarter. And I'll need John to review my auth token changes since they affect the API layer.
[Sarah Chen] (00:03:45): Noted. So we have a dependency chain: staging access -> API spec -> frontend sync -> integration testing. Let's make sure nothing slips. Anything else?
[Alex Rivera] (00:04:00): Just a heads up - the load testing environment has been flaky. If it goes down again, the report will be delayed.
[Sarah Chen] (00:04:15): Flag it immediately if that happens. Don't wait until Thursday to tell me. Alright, let's execute. Daily standups at 9:30 AM. Sprint goal: API migration complete, auth security tickets resolved, dashboard critical flows shipped.
